channel.title: Planning on Sinra
channel.description: Recent content in Planning on Sinra

item.title: The False Promise of User Stories: Why 'As A User' Doesn't Say What To Do
item.description:
url: /planning/2026/01/02/false-promise-user-stories-as-a-user.html

The User Story That Says Nothing

Monday morning. Sprint planning. Team of 6 developers.

Product Owner: “OK, let’s move to the next story.”

The PO reads the story:

USER-347: As a user, I want to be able to log out of the application
to secure my account.

Acceptance Criteria:
- User can log out
- Session is terminated after logout
- User is redirected to login page

PO: “There you go. It’s clear. Questions?”

item.title: Backlog Chaos: When Nobody Knows What's Planned For Q2
item.description:
url: /planning/2025/12/26/backlog-chaos-nobody-knows-q2.html

The Q2 Planning Meeting

Monday morning. Quarterly planning meeting.

CEO: “OK team, what are we shipping in Q2?”

Product Manager: “Let me check the backlog…”

Opens Jira.

Backlog: 537 issues

Available filters:

  • Priority: P0 (12 issues), P1 (47 issues), P2 (89 issues), P3 (145 issues), P4 (244 issues)
  • Status: To Do (412), In Progress (67), Blocked (38), Waiting (20)
  • Labels: 87 different labels (urgent, bug, feature, tech-debt, nice-to-have, critical, MVP, v2, Q1, Q2, maybe, someday, etc.)

PM, overwhelmed:
